news.title: Salman Khan family’s sea-facing Bandra residential project gets MCZMA’s clearance
news.para: Salman Khan's mother, Salma Khan, has received a crucial clearance from the Maharashtra Coastal Zone Management Authority for a new sea-facing residential building in Bandra. The project, a ground-plus-six-storey structure, will be built on a plot previously owned by Mary Clotilda Baptista. The MCZMA emphasized adherence to existing land use and FSI norms from January 18, 2019, for the redevelopment.

news.title: Prestige Group holds ₹65,000 crore unrecognised revenue in books on record pre-sales: Chairman
news.para: Prestige Estates Projects holds a substantial Rs 65,000 crore in unrecognised revenue, fueled by robust housing sales. The company achieved record bookings of Rs 30,024 crore in FY26 and anticipates even stronger performance this fiscal year, targeting Rs 35,000-36,000 crore in sales.  Discussions are underway to adopt a more immediate revenue recognition method.

news.title: Chandigarh allows bed & breakfasts in residential areas
news.para: Chandigarh's residential sectors are set for a transformation as the administration permits Bed & Breakfast (BnB) operations in large homes.  Eligible houses, at least 500 square yards, can now host tourists under a new tourism department policy. This move, however, faces strong opposition from residents concerned about commercialization, increased traffic, and noise.  Officials aim to boost tourism and homeowner income, learning from a previous failed 2008 scheme.

news.title: Embassy Developments to invest ₹1,500 crore to build three million sq ft office space in Bengaluru
news.para: Embassy Developments is investing Rs 1,500 crore to build a 3 million sq ft office complex in Bengaluru, aiming to boost rental income from commercial assets. This move complements their strong residential business, which saw sales bookings more than double last fiscal. The company plans to launch nearly Rs 20,000 crore worth of homes this fiscal, targeting robust consumer demand.

news.title: Prime Hyderabad housing societies’ land caught in decades-old legal maze
news.para: Hyderabad's prime areas like Banjara Hills and Jubilee Hills are embroiled in decades-old land disputes, with thousands of acres under litigation. Overlapping claims, fake survey numbers, and outdated records fuel legal battles between housing societies, private owners, and the state government.  Revenue authorities acknowledge discrepancies, leaving residents in limbo and impacting property transactions.

news.title: Capital gains tax benefit available even if purchase deed for new flat comes later: ITAT
news.para: Taxpayers investing in under-construction or redevelopment projects can now claim capital gains tax relief even if the final deed is delayed. The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that enforceable rights in an identifiable property and timely investment are key. This decision allows for deductions on capital gains, promoting housing investment, and ensures tax benefits are not denied due to procedural delays in property registration.

news.title: Goa's town planning department clears conversion of 11,948 sq meters to settlement zone
news.para: Goa's Town and Country Planning Department has approved the conversion of nearly 12,000 sqm of land, including orchards and natural cover, to settlement zones. These changes, spread across Pernem, Canacona, and Bardez, follow a series of recent alterations to the Regional Plan for Goa 2021.  Approvals are subject to forest department clearances and the outcome of pending High Court petitions.

news.title: Odisha: Integrated IT Park to come up in Damana
news.para: Odisha is set to develop a massive, integrated IT park near Damana Square, aiming to boost the state's digital economy and create jobs. This future-ready hub will feature modern office spaces, co-working areas, residential units, and retail facilities, fostering a self-contained ecosystem.  The project, driven by private participation, will enhance Odisha's standing as an IT destination, with OCAC acting as the nodal agency.

news.title: TGSRTC throws open 694 commercial spaces for lease across Telangana
news.para: Telangana RTC is unlocking new revenue streams by leasing out 694 vacant commercial spaces at bus stations statewide. This significant move aims to leverage high commuter footfall for businesses like eateries and retail outlets. The corporation has published detailed property information online, inviting tenders for these prime locations. Officials anticipate this initiative will boost TGSRTC's finances while providing businesses with excellent market access.

news.title: Tenants in limbo as LDA checklist triggers sealing of private hostels across Lucknow
news.para: Over 100 students in Lucknow are facing homelessness after the Lucknow Development Authority sealed around 20 private hostels for lacking fire safety and violating building norms.  Students like Shivam Kumar and Aman Yadav are struggling to find new, affordable accommodation, with many facing financial losses and disrupted studies. Hostel operators cite prohibitive costs for structural upgrades and fire safety installations as reasons for closure.

news.title: Conveyance for proportionate land areas, says Bombay HC, dismisses housing society’s plea over Andheri plot
news.para: The Bombay High Court has ruled that a cooperative housing society cannot claim excess land due to a mistake in another society's deemed conveyance. Dismissing a petition by Kuber Kartik Society, the court stated it wouldn't facilitate land grabbing. While acknowledging a conveyance isn't final, the HC allowed the petitioner to file a suit if aggrieved by the deputy registrar's order, emphasizing proportionate land distribution.

news.title: Bhopal: Two illegal colonies demolished in Huzur area
news.para: Authorities in Bhopal's Huzur area have razed structures on two parcels of land, dismantling illegal colonies and reclaiming property valued at Rs 27 crore.  One development, on land reportedly belonging to the son of a former IAS officer, was targeted alongside another in Mendori village.  These demolitions address unauthorized construction and plot sales, with 24 such colonies already cleared.

news.title: Mohali: Suntech City's developer in ED custody in ₹172 crore money laundering case
news.para: Realtor Ajay Sehgal has been remanded to three-day Enforcement Directorate custody in a money laundering case linked to the Suntech City project. The ED alleges Sehgal and others developed the project on land they didn't own, using forged documents for permissions, generating over Rs 172 crore in illicit gains. New facts have emerged, necessitating further questioning to trace the money trail and identify other involved individuals.

news.title: Consumer panel orders Ansal Hi-Tech Townships to refund buyer left without flat after 12 years
news.para: A Noida consumer commission has directed Ansal Hi-Tech Townships Limited to refund a homebuyer Rs 1,00,762 plus 6% interest for failing to deliver possession of a flat booked in 2014. The buyer paid instalments over several years but never received the property. The developer did not respond to the complaint, leading to an ex parte order. The commission also awarded Rs 2,000 for litigation costs and mental distress.

news.title: Authorities issue fresh notices to over a dozen commercial establishments after deadly Lucknow fire
news.para: Following a tragic fire that claimed 15 lives, the Lucknow Development Authority intensified its crackdown on commercial buildings violating approved plans. Over a dozen establishments, including gyms, coaching centers, and restaurants, received show-cause notices. This action aims to ensure compliance with building regulations after the devastating Aliganj blaze, with further inspections planned.

news.title: Chhattisgarh housing board earns ₹1,532 crore revenue from sale of 7,388 properties: CM
news.para: Chhattisgarh's Housing Board has achieved a significant turnaround, generating Rs 1,532 crore from property sales over two years. Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai announced the board's expanded role in infrastructure development, aiming to fulfill housing aspirations. The state has also made substantial progress in the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana, completing over 10.50 lakh houses for the needy.

news.title: NGT asks department to take action against 'illegal' groundwater extraction in Noida, Greater Noida
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has directed the Uttar Pradesh Ground Water Department to investigate allegations of illegal groundwater extraction by builders, industries, and individuals in Noida and Greater Noida.  Environmental activist Vikrant Tongad filed a petition after previous complaints to authorities yielded no action. The tribunal mandates ground verification and appropriate remedial and punitive measures if illegal borewells are found, with the process to be completed within three months.
